documents.title: Te Ture Whenua Maori Act 1993 section 2
documents.citation: Te Ture Whenua Maori Act 1993, s 2
documents.text: Section 2 records the purpose of Te Ture Whenua Maori Act 1993, including the retention of Maori land in the hands of its owners, their whanau, and their hapu.

documents.title: Hansard debate on water services entities
documents.citation: New Zealand Parliamentary Debates, Water Services Entities Amendment Bill, 2023
documents.text: Members debated how local authority decision-making and community accountability should apply to water services reform, including the treatment of regional interests.

documents.title: Ellis v R tikanga discussion
documents.text: The Supreme Court discussed tikanga as part of New Zealand law and considered how tikanga principles may bear on the continuation of an appeal.

queries.query_text: Which New Zealand source discusses retaining Maori land for whanau and hapu?

judgements.rationale: The provision directly states the Maori land retention purpose.
The case concerns tikanga but not Maori land retention.

queries.query_text: Find debate material about accountability for water services reform.

judgements.rationale: The Hansard extract directly concerns accountability in water services reform.
The land statute extract is not about water services reform.

queries.query_text: Which case discusses tikanga as part of New Zealand law?

judgements.rationale: The case extract directly identifies tikanga as part of New Zealand law.
The statute is Maori land related but is not the tikanga appeal case.
